steps.title: Read for Heidi's reaction
steps.content: Heidi looks "with wondering eyes" at the pictures, then "screamed aloud" at a peaceful pastoral scene. The follow-up details — "With glowing eyes Heidi devoured the scene" — show the scream is excitement, not fear.

steps.title: State the main idea
steps.content: The text's point is that an image in Grandmama's book captivates and delights Heidi.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Heidi is upset until she sees a serene image': misread; nothing in the text says she was upset before seeing the picture — she was already looking with wonder. ✗
  • Choice B — 'delighted and fascinated by an image': matches "wondering eyes," "glowing eyes," and "devoured the scene." ✓
  • Choice C — 'initially frightened ... but quickly comes to appreciate': misreads the scream as fear; the text gives no fright, only excitement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'because she has never seen a shepherd with his sheep before': overreach; the text never states this is her first time seeing such a scene. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
